task: I’m trying to narrow down a few law schools in the Maryland/DC area and want a practical outreach plan before I start contacting anyone. On the University of Maryland Carey Law site, please find the actual way I can request admissions materials and also look for at least one upcoming online admissions event with its date and time, because I want to see how easy they make it to get information and whether there’s a virtual event I could realistically attend; open the event details page in its own tab and leave it there so I can look at it later. Then go to the University of Baltimore School of Law site and find the instructions for scheduling an admissions meeting by opening the relevant admissions event or meeting page and pulling the registration link or contact method from the actual details page, and keep that page open too so I can compare the two schools side by side. After that, on American University Washington College of Law’s site, find the PIPS Scholarship page and give me a short plain-English summary of what the scholarship is for, along with the application form link, because funding could change which school I prioritize; if there’s a dedicated scholarship page, leave that open in another tab as proof. Once you’ve gathered all of that, recommend which of these three schools I should contact first based on the best mix of easy admissions outreach and potential funding, and include the specific action or contact details you found for each school so I have a simple next move.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully identified the University of Maryland Carey Law admissions-materials request method (the LSAC inquiry form found in step 8) and located an upcoming online admissions event ("Admissions Timelines and Components" on June 10, 2026, at 9:00 AM Pacific / 12:00 PM Eastern, confirmed in step 13). The agent also kept the event details page open in a tab as requested.

R2: success - The agent successfully navigated to the University of Baltimore School of Law's "Visit the School of Law" page (Step 17) and then to the Microsoft Bookings page (Step 18). In Step 19, the agent interacted with the Bookings page, specifically selecting the "JD Online Conversation (Phone/Zoom)" service, which provides the scheduling instructions and registration interface. The agent confirmed this in its final summary, noting the booking link and the fallback contact method (lawadmissions@ubalt.edu, 410-837-4459). The requirement to find the instructions and keep the page open for comparison was met.

R3: success - The agent successfully navigated to the American University Washington College of Law PIPS Scholarship page. In its final response, it provided a clear, plain-English summary of the scholarship ("Full-tuition, three-year scholarship for incoming full-time JD students with strong academic credentials and a public interest or public service career goal") and included the correct application form link (https://american.co1.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_6FMJcuOCtqeRJiu). The agent also kept the relevant pages open as requested.

R4: success - The agent successfully gathered the required information for all three schools:
1. **Maryland Carey Law:** Identified the LSAC inquiry form for materials and found an upcoming webinar (June 10, 2026).
2. **University of Baltimore:** Identified the Microsoft Bookings page for scheduling a "JD Online Conversation" and provided the fallback contact email/phone.
3. **American University WCL:** Identified the PIPS scholarship (full-tuition for public interest/service) and provided the application link.

The agent provided a clear recommendation to contact American WCL first based on the significant funding potential of the PIPS scholarship, and included specific contact/action details for all three schools as requested. The recommendation is well-supported by the gathered evidence.
